summary: A single-story rectangular modern-style building with layered horizontal bands: a white stone base border, a lower wooden plinth, a mid-level stonebrick wall with regular glass windows, a smooth white band above the windows, an upper wooden plank band, and a stone slab coping around a flat roof with an interior wooden floor visible from above.

shape.overall_form: compact rectangular box with a raised flat roof and a surrounding low outer border
shape.symmetry: mostly symmetric along both length and width (mirrored facades on long sides)

materials.name: wood
materials.role: planks bands (upper and lower horizontal bands) and interior floor

materials.name: quartz
materials.role: white outer border and smooth white band (may be smooth quartz blocks)

elements: entrance (small projection visible on one side; uncertain details)
rebuild_hints: Start with a rectangular wooden floor inset by a one-block-wide border of white blocks (quartz-like) around it.
Raise walls of ~3 blocks high: bottom wooden plank trim, then stonebrick band with regularly spaced 1x1 glass windows, then a full white smooth block band, then an upper wooden plank band.
Cap the top with stone slabs as a coping and keep the roof flat and recessed slightly; interior roof area is filled with wood planks.
Place a low white one-block thick outer frame around the building at ground level matching the aerial placement.
uncertainties: Exact material identity of white blocks: appear to be quartz or diorite/smooth stone — uncertain.
Lower corner accent uses cobblestone-like texture on one corner only; unsure if repeated on all corners.
Precise internal features, door placement and exact window spacing dimensions are inferred from visible faces but not fully visible on all sides.
Exact block counts for width/depth/height estimated from images; may be off by a few blocks.
